title: Worth a Walk Ashore
summary: Generic fallback for destinations with a town or waterfront component.
display_text: A stop with a walkable town or waterfront can do more than solve the night’s dockage problem. It can also improve crew morale, which is never a trivial service.
audio_text: A decent walk ashore can improve a crew’s outlook faster than most maintenance projects. That alone gives some towns real value.

title: Useful Stops Matter
summary: Generic fallback for practical shoreside usefulness.
display_text: A stop that offers useful shoreside access can be worth more than pretty views alone. Laundry, groceries, fuel, and an easy walk sometimes make the best argument.
audio_text: Pretty waterfronts are nice, but useful shoreside access often wins the day. Convenience has a way of becoming memorable.
